objective_taxonomy.name: connectivityOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: Whether the relevant parts can still reach one another through usable routes or relationships.

positive: the service stays in one piece
users can still get across the system
avoid_phrases: failure survival as the main evidence
two-endpoint guarantees as the only evidence

boundary_rules: Use this when the key evidence is reachability across a whole system or named subset.
Do not use it when the main evidence is surviving outages; that is robustness.
Do not reduce it to one named pair unless the objective is pairwise connectivity.

confusable_objectives.distinction: If Promote connectivityOf is official, state the floor being preserved and avoid making failure stress the main outcome.
If Promote connectivityOf is official, avoid making a two-endpoint guarantee the only clear cue.

objective_taxonomy.name: robustnessOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: Whether the system keeps working when something breaks, is removed, or comes under stress.

positive: a disruption no longer collapses the plan
stress tests are easier to pass
negative: one failure can stop the plan
the system is brittle under stress
avoid_phrases: single-pair access as the main result

boundary_rules: Use this when failure, outage, removal, or stress survival is central.
Do not use it for ordinary reachability without an explicit disruption condition.
If only two named endpoints are protected, consider pairwise connectivity instead.

confusable_objectives.distinction: If Promote robustnessOf is official, include explicit failure-survival evidence beyond ordinary reachability.
If Promote robustnessOf is official, show the broader system keeps functioning after disruption, not only that two endpoints are better connected.
If Promote robustnessOf is official, state the resilience floor for the broader object, not only a pairwise link.
If Promote robustnessOf is official, include disruption survival rather than only group tightness.

objective_taxonomy.name: centralityOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: How much one entity or relationship acts as the key hub, broker, bottleneck, or point of influence.

positive: one actor becomes the main broker
negative: work no longer depends on one actor
influence is less concentrated on one point
avoid_phrases: overall evenness as the main evidence
group tightness as the main evidence

boundary_rules: Use this when the story is about a named entity or relationship gaining or losing prominence.
Do not use it when the main point is broad even distribution; that is balance.
Do not use it for hierarchy unless ranking or command layers are the main signal.

confusable_objectives.distinction: If Reduce centralityOf is official, focus on the focal entity's reduced prominence, not broad distribution.
If Promote centralityOf is official, focus on one entity gaining importance rather than global imbalance.
If Promote centralityOf is official, focus on the focal entity rather than whole-system tiers.

objective_taxonomy.name: densityOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: How packed or sparse the relationships are within the relevant whole system or subset.

positive: the area becomes more tightly linked
negative: the subset is deliberately kept sparse
avoid_phrases: community separation as the main evidence

boundary_rules: Use this when the amount of relationship coverage is the main evidence.
Do not use it when local triangle-like closure is the point; that is clustering.
Do not use it when the point is separating communities; that is modularity.

confusable_objectives.distinction: If Promote densityOf is official, distinguish link abundance from group unity.
If Promote densityOf is official, keep the cue about overall packedness, not local triangles.
If Promote densityOf is official, distinguish packedness from raw growth.

objective_taxonomy.name: cohesivenessOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: How strongly a group holds together internally and resists being pulled apart.

positive: the group becomes harder to split
the unit holds together under pressure
negative: the unit can split apart more easily
coordination inside the group is reduced
avoid_phrases: simple reachability as the only evidence
failure survival as the main evidence

boundary_rules: Use this when internal bonding or togetherness is the main signal.
Do not use it for mere reachability; that is connectivity.
Do not use it for disruption survival unless the story emphasizes stress tolerance.

confusable_objectives.distinction: If Promote cohesivenessOf is official, focus on group binding rather than link counts.
If Promote cohesivenessOf is official, keep the evidence inside one group.
If Promote cohesivenessOf is official, focus on group unity rather than disruption survival.

objective_taxonomy.name: clusteringOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: Whether local neighborhoods close up around the same participants, making nearby partners also connected to each other.

positive: local partners are connected to one another
negative: nearby partners no longer all connect
avoid_phrases: overall packedness as the only evidence
large community separation as the main evidence

boundary_rules: Use this when the evidence is local closure among nearby participants.
Do not use it for general relationship density across an entire system.
Do not use it for community partition quality; that is modularity.

confusable_objectives.distinction: If Promote clusteringOf is official, focus on local neighborhoods rather than whole-object packedness.
If Promote clusteringOf is official, do not make community partition quality the main result.

objective_taxonomy.name: sizeOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: How many relevant entities or relationships are included in the whole system or selected subset.
boundary_rules: Use this when count or scope expansion/contraction is the main evidence.
Do not use it for stronger or weaker relationships unless the number of items changes.
Do not confuse size with centrality, which is about prominence rather than count.

confusable_objectives.distinction: If Promote sizeOf is official, make count growth explicit rather than packedness.

objective_taxonomy.name: modularityOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: How clearly the system separates into internally focused communities with fewer cross-community ties.

positive: cross-group ties are reduced while internal ties remain

boundary_rules: Use this when community separation or partition quality is central.
Do not use it for local closure around one participant; that is clustering.
Do not use it for generic packedness; that is density.

confusable_objectives.distinction: If Promote modularityOf is official, focus on community partition quality, not local triangles.
If Promote modularityOf is official, emphasize the partition, not a single group's unity.

objective_taxonomy.name: balanceOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: How evenly load, responsibility, attention, or connection is spread rather than concentrated.

positive: no one site carries the burden alone
negative: responsibility is pulled back to fewer actors
avoid_phrases: one named hub losing prominence as the only evidence

boundary_rules: Use this when broad evenness of distribution is the main signal.
Do not use it when evidence is only one entity losing prominence; that is centrality.
Do not use it for failure survival unless the even spread is the explicit objective.

confusable_objectives.distinction: If Promote balanceOf is official, describe system-wide evenness rather than only one bottleneck.
If Reduce balanceOf is official, describe distribution becoming less even across the system, not only one focal entity.
If Reduce balanceOf is official, focus on unevenness rather than formal layers.

objective_taxonomy.name: assortativityOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: Whether similar entities preferentially connect or work with similar entities.

avoid_phrases: pairwise similarity between two named items only

boundary_rules: Use this when similarity-based pairing across the system is the point.
Do not use it for two specific entities being similar; that is pairwise similarity.
Do not use it for communities unless the boundary is explicitly based on similarity.

confusable_objectives.distinction: If Promote assortativityOf is official, keep the cue graph-wide rather than pairwise.
If Reduce assortativityOf is official, describe the mixing tendency across the graph, not one pair's similarity.

objective_taxonomy.name: hierarchyOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: How strongly relationships form layers, ranks, command chains, or upstream-downstream order.

positive: a clearer chain of command appears
upstream and downstream roles are separated

boundary_rules: Use this when layered order or rank structure is the main evidence.
Do not use it for a single influential entity unless layers are involved.
Do not use it for broad distribution of load; that is balance.

confusable_objectives.distinction: If Promote hierarchyOf is official, make layered ordering the main outcome.

objective_taxonomy.name: distanceBetween
objective_taxonomy.definition: How many steps, handoffs, or units of effort separate two named endpoints.

positive: the two endpoints are kept farther apart
the route between them becomes longer
negative: the two endpoints are brought closer

boundary_rules: Use this when the distance or number of steps between two named endpoints is central.
Do not use it for path capacity or redundancy; that is pairwise connectivity.
Do not use it for overall system reachability.

confusable_objectives.distinction: If Reduce distanceBetween is official, focus on route length rather than path quality.

objective_taxonomy.name: connectivityBetween
objective_taxonomy.definition: How well two named endpoints can reach each other through available, dependable, or high-capacity options.

positive: the two endpoints gain a dependable connection
fallback access between the pair is preserved
negative: the pair has fewer access options
capacity between the endpoints is reduced
a fallback between them is removed
avoid_phrases: failure survival for the whole system
shorter distance without capacity or availability

boundary_rules: Use this when the evidence names two endpoints and their mutual access improves or is preserved.
Do not use it for whole-system reachability; that is connectivity.
Do not use it for merely shortening steps unless availability or capacity also matters.

confusable_objectives.distinction: If Promote connectivityBetween is official, keep the evidence pairwise and do not make whole-system failure tolerance the main result.
If Promote connectivityBetween is official, keep the guarantee between the named pair rather than across the whole system.
If Promote connectivityBetween is official, keep the guarantee pairwise.
If Promote connectivityBetween is official, avoid making shortest path length the main outcome.

objective_taxonomy.name: similarityBetween
objective_taxonomy.definition: How much two named entities play similar roles, face similar conditions, or connect in similar ways.

positive: two entities are made more interchangeable
they can be compared as peers
negative: one is given a distinct pattern
avoid_phrases: same-type pairing across the whole system

boundary_rules: Use this when two named entities become more or less role-equivalent.
Do not use it for system-wide similar-with-similar pairing; that is assortativity.
Do not use it for the pair being directly connected or closer.

confusable_objectives.distinction: If Promote similarityBetween is official, keep the cue pairwise.
If Promote similarityBetween is official, keep the evidence pairwise rather than graph-wide.

node_meaning.prompt: What does one node represent in this story?

edge_meaning.prompt: What does one edge or relationship represent?
